identity.name: Teacher
identity.definition: A Teacher is an educator associated with one or more schools and classes who creates and delivers instructional content, assigns and monitors student work, administers assessments, and analyzes learning data to make informed instructional decisions across Renaissance products.

representations.heading: Card layout
representations.description: How a Teacher appears in grids and dashboards

attributes.name: Full Name
attributes.description: Primary identifier. Appears everywhere a Teacher is shown.

attributes.name: Role/Title
attributes.description: Job title or teaching position. Determines display context.

attributes.name: Profile Photo
attributes.description: Visual identifier. Max 2MB, JPG/PNG. Default: initials avatar.

attributes.name: School(s)
attributes.description: Which school(s) the teacher is assigned to.

attributes.name: Subject Area(s)
attributes.description: Teaching subject area(s). Drives product recommendations and content filtering.

attributes.name: Class(es)
attributes.description: Which classes the teacher is assigned to.

attributes.name: Full Name
attributes.description: Primary identifier. Appears everywhere a Teacher is shown.

attributes.name: Email
attributes.description: Primary contact and notification address. Must be unique within district.

attributes.name: School(s)
attributes.description: Which school(s) the teacher is assigned to.

attributes.name: Class(es)
attributes.description: Which classes the teacher is assigned to.

attributes.name: Role/Title
attributes.description: Job title or teaching position. Determines display context.

attributes.name: Subject Area(s)
attributes.description: Teaching subject area(s). Drives product recommendations and content filtering.

attributes.name: Grade Band
attributes.description: Grade levels the teacher works with. Inferred from class assignments or set manually.

attributes.name: Employee ID
attributes.description: Unique system identifier. Used for rostering, data matching, and SIS sync.

attributes.name: Product Access
attributes.description: Which Renaissance products the teacher is licensed to use.

attributes.name: Username / Login
attributes.description: How the teacher authenticates. Unique within org.

attributes.name: Profile Photo
attributes.description: Visual identifier. Max 2MB, JPG/PNG. Default: initials avatar.

attributes.name: District
attributes.description: The district the teacher belongs to.

attributes.name: Phone
attributes.description: Contact phone number. Optional, not displayed in product UI.

attributes.name: Certifications
attributes.description: Teaching certifications and endorsements. Informational only.

attributes.name: Preferred Language
attributes.description: UI language preference. Default: English.

stories.title: View Class Dashboard
stories.benefit: I can monitor student activity, track assignment completion, and review recent assessment results for my class.
stories.whenClause: I navigate to one of my Classes,
stories.thenClause: a dashboard showing student activity, assignment progress, and recent assessment results.

stories.title: Create and Assign Work
stories.benefit: students receive targeted practice aligned to their skill levels and curricular goals.
stories.whenClause: I create an Assignment for my Class,
stories.thenClause: the assignment is distributed to enrolled students with targeted skills and due dates.

stories.title: Analyze Student Progress
stories.benefit: I can identify students who need intervention and adjust my instruction accordingly.
stories.whenClause: I view Score reports for my Students,
stories.thenClause: aggregated and individual score data across assessments, with trend indicators and benchmark comparisons.

businessRules.title: Uniqueness
businessRules.description: A Teacher is uniquely identified within a District by their Employee ID and email address. Both must be unique.

businessRules.title: Creation
businessRules.description: Teachers are primarily created via SIS roster sync (OneRoster, Clever, ClassLink). Manual creation requires at minimum: First Name, Last Name, Email, Role/Title.

businessRules.title: Class Assignment
businessRules.description: A Teacher must be assigned to at least one Class to access student data and instructional tools. Teachers without class assignments see only their profile and settings.

businessRules.title: Data Scope
businessRules.description: Teachers can only view data for Students enrolled in their own Classes. Cross-class visibility requires Admin privileges.

businessRules.title: Product Licensing
businessRules.description: A Teacher's available product features are determined by their Product Access licenses. Unlicensed products are hidden from the teacher's view.

businessRules.title: Multi-School
businessRules.description: A Teacher may be assigned to multiple Schools within a District. Each school assignment grants access to classes and students at that school.

businessRules.title: Deactivation
businessRules.description: Deactivated teachers lose product access and are hidden from active staff lists. Their historical data (assignments created, reports generated) is retained.

states.name: Active
states.description: Currently employed and using the platform
states.triggers: SIS sync import, manual creation, reactivation

states.name: Inactive
states.description: Temporarily not using the platform (e.g. leave of absence)
states.triggers: SIS sync status change, admin action

states.name: Transferred
states.description: Moving between schools within the district
states.triggers: SIS sync or admin action; temporary state during school reassignment

states.name: Deactivated
states.description: No longer employed; account disabled, data retained

relationships.mechanics: Teachers are assigned to Classes via SIS roster sync (automated) or by Admins manually. A teacher is the primary instructor for their assigned classes.
relationships.cardinality: One-to-many. A Teacher can teach multiple Classes; each Class has one primary Teacher. Typical: 1-8 classes per teacher.
relationships.sorts: Classes for a Teacher: alphabetical by class name (default), by grade level, by subject area, by period/schedule.
relationships.filters: By subject area, by grade level, by school (for multi-school teachers), by active/archived status.
relationships.dependencies: If a Class is archived, the teacher loses access to that class's student data. If a teacher is deactivated, their classes must be reassigned.

relationships.mechanics: Teachers are assigned to Schools via SIS roster sync. Multi-school assignments are supported for district-level staff.
relationships.cardinality: Many-to-many. A Teacher can be assigned to multiple Schools; a School has many Teachers. Typical: 1-3 schools per teacher.
relationships.sorts: Teachers within a School: alphabetical by last name (default), by role/title, by subject area.
relationships.filters: By role/title, by subject area, by grade band, by active/inactive status.
relationships.dependencies: If a School is deactivated, teachers must be transferred to another School or deactivated.

relationships.mechanics: Teachers access Students indirectly through Class enrollment. A teacher sees students enrolled in their classes.
relationships.cardinality: Many-to-many (via Class). A Teacher sees all Students in their Classes; a Student may have multiple Teachers. Typical: 20-150 students per teacher.
relationships.sorts: Students for a Teacher: alphabetical by last name (default), by grade level, by reading/math level, by class.
relationships.filters: By class, by grade level, by proficiency level, by enrollment status, by demographic group.
relationships.dependencies: If a Student is removed from all of a Teacher's classes, the teacher loses visibility of that student's data.

nestedObjects.name: Assignment
nestedObjects.description: Teacher creates assignments for their classes

structure.evidence: A Teacher has a rich set of attributes: name, email, employee ID, role/title, subject areas, grade band, school, classes, certifications, product access, and more. You can design a detail page for a Teacher.

instances.evidence: Ms. Rodriguez (3rd Grade ELA teacher at Lincoln Elementary), Mr. Chen (Math teacher at Washington Middle), Dr. Williams (Science teacher at Jefferson High). Every school has dozens of Teacher instances.

purpose.evidence: Teachers are the primary power users across all Renaissance products. They create assignments, administer assessments, analyze student data, and make instructional decisions. The entire platform is designed around empowering teachers.

sipValidation.verdict: Core system object. The primary actor/user across all Renaissance products.

synonyms.notes: Used in broader contexts that include coaches and specialists
Less common in K-12 Renaissance products
Broader term that includes non-teaching staff
Generic term in technical contexts; not used in product UI

objectViews.description: Teachers at a school listed with their role, subjects, and class assignments. Admins browse this to check staffing and jump to individual profiles.
A minimal row for selecting which teacher to assign to a class. Shows name, role, photo, and subject area — just enough to pick the right person.
The complete teacher profile — contact info, school and class assignments, product access, and administrative controls. Admins manage teacher accounts and permissions from here.
Teachers listed in an admin data table for bulk account management. Focused on role, school assignment, and account status with quick edit and deactivation controls.
The teacher's name displayed inline within a report to attribute results. Purely informational — no interactive elements.
The teacher shown as part of a class banner — name, role, and photo identifying who teaches this class.
A summary card showing role, subjects, classes, and school at a glance. Used in dashboards where admins need a quick overview of a teacher.
